content: The spelling variant 'reflexion' (the older, less common alternative spelling of 'reflection') that exists alongside the standard 'reflection' can be framed as a natural, linguistic precursor to multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Just as LLM self-reflection benefits from contrasting inconsistent solving perspectives to improve outputs, drawing on the dual spellings 'reflexion' and 'reflection' creates an inherent small contrast that can prime LLMs to engage in more critical, divergent self-reflection during reasoning tasks.
